name: Berliner Unterwelten (Subterranean Berlin)
description: A tour of underground Berlin takes you through WWII air raid shelters and tunnels used to smuggle people out of East Germany.

name: Liquidrom
description: This futuristic German spa allows visitors to float in a pool of salt water while zoning out to underwater techno.

name: Monsterkabinett
description: A whimsical and twisted animatronic monster tour beneath the streets of Berlin.

name: Berlin Botanical Garden
description: Started as a kitchen garden, now the second largest botanical garden in the world.

name: Tajikistan Tearoom
description: A slice of central Asia in the middle of Berlin.

name: Pergamon Museum
description: The most visited, and possibly most controversial, museum in Germany.

name: Design Panoptikum
description: A surreal museum of extraordinary industrial objects.

name: Stasi Museum
description: A museum dedicated to the history of a terrifying secret police is now held in their former headquarters.

name: Haus Schwarzenberg Street Art Alley
description: A narrow passage next to a cafe leads to a courtyard exploding with murals, tags, and paste-ups.

name: Museum of Unheard-of Things
description: A wunderkammer of mysterious objects and stories.

name: Memorial to the Murdered Jews of Europe
description: Thousands of coffin-like pillars make up this controversial Holocaust memorial.

name: Burgermeister
description: Where once there was a train station bathroom, there is now this German burger joint.

name: Hall of Mirrors in Clärchens Ballhaus
description: This classic restaurant features a gorgeous mirror-clad room.

name: Vestiges of the Berlin Wall
description: On the Spree bank, discover one of the last reminders of a symbolic wall that divided, and was torn down by the people.

name: The DDR Museum
description: A fascinating insight into daily life in the Eastern Bloc.

name: Gärten der Welt (Gardens of the World)
description: Gardens representing parts of Asia, Europe, and the Mid East are tucked away among the Soviet-style buildings in East Berlin.

name: Teufelsberg
description: An abandoned NSA listening post on Berlin's "Devil's Mountain."

name: Pfaueninsel
description: This Berlin island is home to a flock of free-roaming peacocks, as well as a historic ivory folly.

name: Buchstabenmuseum
description: A collection of abandoned typographic signs from Berlin and beyond.

name: Berlin Story Bunker
description: This World War II bunker now survives as a documentation of the Nazi regime and war crimes.

name: Museum für Naturkunde
description: Berlin's natural history museum houses the world's largest mounted dinosaur and the late beloved polar bear Knut.

name: Führerbunker Parking Lot
description: The bunker where Hitler took his own life is hidden under this deliberately ordinary parking lot.

name: Museum der Dinge
description: From toys to kettles to scientific instruments, this German museum celebrates the beauty of 20th century design.

name: The Bebelplatz
description: The site of the famous Nazi book burning campaign of 1933 remembers its "Night of Shame".

name: Café Strauss
description: Residents of Berlin take their coffee and cake in one of the city’s most restful spaces: a cemetery.

name: Magicum
description: Visitors can explore the role of mysticism throughout the ages inside this interactive museum of magic.

name: David Hasselhoff Museum
description: What started as a tongue-in-cheek shrine is now a full-blown museum celebrating "The Hoff" and his longtime love affair with Berlin.

name: Berliner Medizinhistorisches Museum
description: Home to 10,000 medical specimens including the remains of Rudolf Virchow's extensive collection of pathological specimens.

name: The Bierpinsel
description: A tree-like building perches on its trunk in the Steglitz neighborhood of Berlin.

name: 'Stand By Me' Tree
description: Someone loved this song enough to carve all of its lyrics onto a tree trunk in a Berlin park.

name: Wir Waren Nachbarn (We Were Neighbors)
description: A unique Holocaust memorial tells the personal stories of the Jewish residents exiled from greater Berlin.

name: Spandauer Zitadelle (Spandau Citadel)
description: A 12th-century fortress containing some of Berlin's oldest buildings.

name: Hohenzollern Crypt in the Berlin Cathedral
description: Beneath the impressive baroque cathedral that rises from the center of Berlin lies the largest collection of Prussian royals in the world.

name: Schwerbelastungskörper
description: Super-heavy Nazi architectural experiment.

name: Hatch Sticker Museum
description: This little museum is making sure that the art and history of the graffiti sticker is not scraped from history.

name: Soviet Graffiti in the Reichstag
description: The walls of Germany's Parliament still show the scars of the Red Army takeover in World War II.

name: Prinzessinnengarten
description: Berliners converted a bombed-out, disused lot into a community-run urban farm with its own cafe and bar.

name: Humboldthain Flak Tower
description: These former Nazi anti-aircraft towers offer a singular view of the north of Berlin in the summer, and a sanctuary for bats in winter.

name: Badeschiff
description: Floating swimming pool within a moored vessel.

name: Tempelhof Airport
description: Berlin's old airport.

name: Kaninchenfeld (Rabbit Field)
description: These brass bunnies honor the rabbits that dominated the no-man's-land around the Berlin Wall.

name: Fliegeberg
description: This conical hill was built as a testing ground for an engineer known as the Glider King.

name: Old City Wall of Berlin
description: The last remnants of a massive medieval wall that once encompassed the city of Berlin.

name: Berlin Radio Tower
description: A restaurant and observation deck built in a unique broadcasting antenna.

name: Hansa Studios
description: From Iggy Pop to David Bowie to Nick Cave, this regal music studio has served some of the all time greats.

name: 'The Dance of Death'
description: At one of Berlin’s oldest churches, visitors can place mosaic tiles to help restore a medieval mural.

name: Marx and Engels Forum
description: One of downtown Berlin's last reminders of its Communist past.

name: Georg-von-Rauch-Haus
description: Named after a militant urban guerilla, this famous former squat is a graffiti-covered stronghold of leftist culture.

name: Puppentheater-Museum Berlin
description: Puppet theater and museum holding a collection of more than 300.

name: Abandoned Iraqi Embassy
description: When German-Iraqi relations broke down, this embassy was vacated post haste.

name: Ramones Museum
description: Blitzkrieg bop your way through Berlin's Ramones Museum.

name: German-Russian Museum
description: A museum in the building where Nazi Germany signed its surrender tells the story of World War II from the Soviet perspective.

name: Mengenlehreuhr (Berlin Clock)
description: The world's first clock to use set theory as a measure of time required the invention of a new light bulb just to maintain its obtuse measurements.

name: Tieranatomisches Theater
description: Berlin's oldest surviving academic architecture hosted decades of animal dissections.

name: The 'Hand with Watch' Sculpture
description: Known simply as "The Hand," this famous public sculpture in Berlin was featured in a Depeche Mode music video.

name: Velvet
description: Taste freshly foraged yarrow and black locust blossoms at this ambitious cocktail bar.

name: Denkzeichen Georg Elser
description: A monument to the man who almost killed Hitler.

name: Berlin-Friedrichstrasse Railway Station
description: Cold war icon and onetime "hole" in the iron curtain.

name: Friedrichsfelde Socialist Cemetery
description: The final resting place of many of Germany's most famous communists.

name: Wasserturm Prenzlauer Berg
description: This water-tower-turned-high-end-apartment building was once the site of an early Nazi concentration camp.

name: Bridge of Spies
description: Germany's Glienicke Bridge has a shady history of clandestine meetings, espionage, and spycraft.

name: Dahlem-Dorf U-Bahn Station Benches
description: A traditional U-Bahn station in Berlin gets a string of rather untraditional benches.

name: Schöneberg Gasometer
description: The skeletal frame of a decommissioned gas-pumping station offers breathtaking views of greater Berlin.

name: Schwarzes Café
description: Dine at a legendary after-hours joint frequented by David Bowie and Iggy Pop.

name: Stadtbahn Viaduct World War II Damage
description: Reminders of the Battle of Berlin still scar the railway.

name: Otto Weidt's Workshop for the Blind
description: A brush maker used his factory to protect blind Jews from the Gestapo.

name: Stolpersteine Holocaust Memorials
description: Tiny memorials honor individual victims of the Holocaust.

name: Carillon Berlin-Tiergarten
description: One of the world's heaviest musical instruments towers within a popular urban park.

name: Fine Bagels
description: The best bagels in Berlin are tucked away in an independent bookstore.

name: Konditorei Buchwald
description: For more than 170 years, this family-run bakery has been serving Berlin’s best Baumkuchen, or “tree cake.”

name: Memorial to Homosexuals Persecuted Under Nazism
description: A memorial in the Tiergarten is a reminder of the suffering inflicted on homosexuals by the Nazi regime.

name: Oberhafenkantine Berlin
description: A portable, wooden replica (minus the hyphen) of Hamburg’s iconic crooked coffee shop.

name: Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church
description: An imperial church bombed to ruins during WWII now stands as a jagged memorial.

name: Friede sei mit Dir
description: This obscene artwork on the side of a newspaper office pokes fun at a rival paper's former editor-in-chief.

name: Berlin Victory Column
description: A monument to the three wars that led to German unification towers within a Berlin traffic circle.

name: Classic Remise
description: An old tram depot transformed into a vintage car paradise.

name: Luisenfriedhof II
description: One of the oldest cemeteries in Germany was originally used for victims of a cholera epidemic.

name: Artpark Tegel
description: This concrete residential area doubles as an open-air gallery.

name: Babka & Kranz
description: Superb sweets await at the first Jewish bakery inducted into Berlin’s prestigious baker’s guild.

name: Silent Green Kulturquartier
description: Concerts, art, offices, and a café in what used to be Berlin's first crematorium.

name: Prussian National Monument for the Liberation Wars
description: A towering monument to war, located on a beautifully peaceful hill.

name: Bahnhof Berlin Zoologischer Garten
description: Metro station with a claim to rock n' roll fame.

name: Knut the Polar Bear
description: Berlin's beloved bear is immortalized at the Museum für Naturkunde.

name: Weißensee Cemetery
description: This sprawling Jewish cemetery is one of the largest in Europe.

name: DDR Watch Tower
description: This watchtower once presided over the Berlin Wall.

name: The Bendlerblock
description: After a nearly successful attempt on Hitler's life, the conspirators met their fate in the courtyard of this Berlin building.

name: Tegel Creek Water Buffaloes
description: The non-native bovine herd was brought in to help conserve these Berlin wetlands.

name: Natur-Park Südgelände
description: Art installations, derelict railcars, and endangered grasshoppers all call this south Berlin park home.

name: Messedamm Underpass
description: You've probably seen these distinctive orange-tiled columns on the big screen.

name: Malakeh
description: After fleeing the Syrian civil war, chef Malakeh Jazmati began serving exceptional renditions of her homeland's cuisine in Berlin.

name: 'Molecule Man'
description: This monumental work of art symbolizes the coming together of a previously divided city.

name: Platform 17 Memorial
description: This platform is now a memorial dedicated to those who were deported during the Holocaust.

name: German Resistance Memorial Center
description: The site where a plot to assassinate Hitler was planned—and its conspirators were executed—is now a powerful memorial to the German resistance during World War II.

name: Schrotkugelturm (Shot Ball Tower)
description: A century-old shot ball factory tower is open to the public once a year.

name: Freedom Bell
description: A replica of the Liberty Bell rang daily from West Berlin's City Hall throughout the Cold War.

name: Schönhauser Allee Cemetery
description: Inside this Jewish cemetery is a memorial to soldiers who deserted the German army and were executed.

name: The Frame Collector's Museum
description: Antique frame collection with 2,400 frames.

name: Judengang
description: Once a path for Jewish funeral processions, this unassuming alleyway is rich in local lore.

name: Fernsehturm Müggelberge
description: This TV tower once secretly housed a GDR intelligence team.

name: 'Das Schicksal Der (Fate of) Bravenleavanne'
description: The site of good faith and good nature in the fictional world of Kcymaerxthaere.

name: Archenhold Observatory
description: This public observatory is home to the "celestial cannon," the longest pointable telescope in the world.

name: Onkel Toms Hütte (Uncle Tom's Cabin)
description: The 1852 novel "Uncle Tom's Cabin" lends its name to a subway station, a street, and several shops on the edge of Berlin.

name: Soviet Memorial (Sowjetisches Ehrenmal)
description: For patriotic Russians, this memorial honoring the Soviet soldiers who died liberating Berlin in 1945 is the most sacred location outside of Russia.

name: Damaskus Konditorei
description: A Syrian family keeps memories of their homeland alive at this sweet shop.

name: Memorial to the Sinti and Roma Victims of National Socialism
description: Dedicated to the European Sinti and Roma peoples murdered during the Holocaust.

name: ‘Trains to Life – Trains to Death’
description: A haunting contrast of trains that both saved and took the lives of Jewish children in Nazi Germany.

name: Moses Mendelssohn Memorial
description: The original burial place of this preeminent 18th-century Jewish philosopher was razed by the Nazis.

name: The Clock of Flowing Time
description: An astonishingly accurate water clock that doubles as an hourly spectacle in this Berlin shopping area.

name: The Trabi Museum
description: A museum specifically dedicated to the Trabant, one of the iconic automobiles of the GDR.

name: Herta Heuwer Currywurst Memorial
description: A plaque on the side of a building is a small tribute to the woman who invented an iconic Berlin fast food.

name: Haus des Lehrers
description: A socialist building dedicated to teachers showcases one of the largest Soviet mosaics in the world.

name: Alliierten Museum
description: This museum is home to a sugar sculpture of the Berlin Airlift Memorial.

name: Nilpferdbrunnen
description: A small hippo-shaped fountain in Berlin, Germany.

name: Sternenwächter
description: A statue of an astronomer gazes at the sky as a tribute to Caroline Herschel.

name: Gründer Geist
description: Keep your eyes out for this "ghost" and walking pun.

name: 'Buttbrunnen'
description: Largely overlooked, this little fountain on Museum Island is a whimsical tribute to a Prussian king nicknamed the “Fatty Flounder.”

name: Feuermelder
description: A historic fire call point.

name: Hansaviertel
description: In the summer of 1957, a massive architectural exhibition showed off the future of urban living in this German neighborhood.

name: Pressecafé Mural
description: A prime example of socialist modernist architecture, wrapped in a enormous fresco about the media.

name: Käthe Paulus Memorial
description: A plaque marks the former home of Käthe Paulus, an aerial acrobat who invented the modern folding parachute.

name: Bud Spencer Museum
description: Celebrating the works of a beloved Italian actor, known for such cult films as 'They Call Me Trinity' and 'Watch Out, We’re Mad!'

name: Şengüloğlu Baklava
description: This confectioner serves one of the finest renditions of Antep baklava outside of Turkey.

name: Urban Nation
description: Ever-changing museum dedicated to graffiti and contemporary street art.

name: Unité d'Habitation of Berlin
description: After local authorities changed the design, the building's architect, Le Corbusier, wanted nothing to do with it.

name: The Suicide Cemetery
description: A hidden, morbidly fascinating cemetery deep in the forest.

name: AVUS-Tribüne (Automobile Traffic and Practice Road)
description: The world's first motorway was used as a racetrack until the late 1990s.

name: The Tale of the Scale and the Skull
description: This sprawling stone mosaic on Berlin’s Museum Island transforms archaeological finds and local legends into a walkable work of art.

name: Berlin Coin Cabinet
description: One of the largest numismatic collections in Europe fell victim to a huge theft.

name: Weltzeituhr
description: Check the time in 145 countries at once with this East German relic.

name: Ferry Line F24
description: The most peculiar part of Berlin's public transit network is an on-demand rowboat.

name: Ernst Thälmann Monument
description: A massive relic of the communist era of Berlin.

name: Steinstücken Exclave
description: A helicopter memorial commemorating what was once the only way to access this West Berlin exclave.

name: Fischerpinte
description: Relaxed riverfront dive bar where you can also rent a boat.

name: Nightcaps
description: A series of sculptures, “sitting” directly on top of Berlin's existing street lamps, addressing the overlooked topic of urban light pollution.

name: Memorial SA Prison Papestraße
description: Early Nazi prison for political prisoners.

name: Aufbau der Republik
description: One of the best-preserved pieces of DDR propaganda in Berlin.

name: The Parliament of Trees
description: A curious memorial to the Berlin Wall, German division, the war, and those who died at the wall.

name: The Swallowed Urn
description: Where a hospital garden hides a haunting reminder of life’s cycle.

name: Neue Wache
description: A poignant memorial that traces two hundred years of German history.

name: Boros Collection
description: Art and concrete merge into a single entity within a WWII bunker that possesses a deeply storied and turbulent history.

name: The Pink Tube
description: An intersection between architecture, industrial construction, machine, and scientific instrument.

name: Mäusebunker
description: Along the banks of the Teltow Canal in southwestern Berlin stands one of the city’s most controversial buildings, a hulking Brutalist laboratory that looks more like a warship than a research facility.

name: Gececondu of Kreuzberg
description: An Anatolian immigrant created his own little paradise in the shadow of the Berlin Wall, made possible by the tensions of the Cold War.

name: Bent Metal on Two Cars
description: Likely the world’s only place where you’re meant to skate on top of cars.

name: The Golden Rift
description: A thin golden line commemorates the deadliest terrorist attack in recent German history.

name: Make Art Not War Mural
description: An important message and optimistic splash of color in a troubled neighborhood.

name: Spreepark
description: An eerie, derelict amusement park in Berlin.

name: The Return of the Cows
description: Art and a pun, all in one.

name: AquaDom
description: The largest free-standing aquarium in the world resides in a hotel lobby in Berlin.

name: Weissensee Abandoned Children's Hospital
description: A state-of-the-art medical facility from the Imperial era is now a derelict ruin.

name: Peristal Singum
description: Fall down the rabbit hole into a surreal journey of human consciousness "from ass to soul."

name: Ballhaus Riviera
description: The music is over at the Ballhaus Riviera Ballroom, but the ballroom remains in haunting disrepair.

name: Hubertusbad
description: An abandoned swimming pool decays on the outskirts of Berlin.

name: Deutsches Currywurst Museum
description: A museum dedicated to a Cold War era sausage.

name: Arthouse Tacheles
description: An old department store turned Nazi prison turned artist commune.

name: Böhmisches Brauhaus
description: The abandoned remains of this Victorian brewery hide crumbling bowling alleys and rusting industrial gear.

name: Propeller Island City Lodge
description: Come stay at a live-in art installation.

name: Off-World Gaming
description: A dilapidated space station with overtones of 'Alien' and 'Bladerunner' featuring a multiplayer games galore.

name: Zucker-Museum
description: A sweet museum focused on beets.

name: Wunderkammer Olbricht at me Collectors Room
description: A remarkable cabinet of curiosities in an art space in the center of Berlin.
